summary: Current upper bound is 3.75%. Market expects ~65% chance of no change through April, ~30% chance of one 25bp cut, small tails for two cuts or hike.
content:
Current state: Upper bound at 3.75% since January 2026 FOMC hold.

FOMC meetings before April 30: March 17-18, April 28-29.

Key market signals:
- CME FedWatch (Feb 11): 94.1% probability of March hold
- Polymarket "cut by April meeting": 38% probability
- Polymarket "Fed decisions Mar-Jun": Pause-Pause-Cut 38%, Pause-Pause-Pause 24%, Pause-Cut-Pause 11%, Cut-Pause-Pause 9.8%
- Kalshi April meeting: ~80% above 3.50%, ~12% above 3.75%
- rateprobability.com: April implied rate 3.43%, -21 bps

Analyst views:
- Goldman/Barclays: postponed cuts to Sep/Dec 2026
- BofA: two cuts in June/July → 3-3.25%
- JPM: no cuts in 2026
- iShares/BlackRock: rates down to ~3% over 2026

Strong January jobs report (Feb 11) dramatically reduced near-term cut expectations. March cut dropped from 22% to 5-6%.
